title: Causal TMS Study: Thalamic and Insular Gating of Ignition Threshold
description: Single-pulse TMS is applied over the right posterior insula and the mediodorsal thalamus (via fMRI-guided neuronavigation) to test whether these structures causally regulate the APGI ignition threshold θₜ. APGI predicts that insula TMS selectively disrupts interoceptive gating (reducing Πⁱ_eff and raising θₜ for interoceptive but not exteroceptive streams), while thalamic TMS affects global ignition probability (Bₜ) across both streams. The Perturbational Complexity Index (PCI) serves as a validated ignition proxy. Three sub-predictions are tested: insula/thalamus regulate θₜ causally (P2a), TMS of insula selectively disrupts interoceptive gating while sparing exteroceptive P3b (P2b), and thalamic TMS shifts Bₜ globally across both streams (P2c). CAUTION: Thalamic gating is one plausible biological implementation of threshold regulation; cortical inhibitory mechanisms and claustrum involvement remain viable alternatives. Paper caption must note this explicitly.

sub_predictions.claim: Insula and thalamus regulate θₜ causally
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: PCI reduced by insula TMS; HEP–PCI correlation abolished under active stimulation
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No threshold change following active TMS vs. sham

sub_predictions.claim: TMS of insula selectively disrupts interoceptive gating
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: HEP–P3b coupling abolished under insula TMS; exteroceptive P3b amplitude spared
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Uniform suppression of both interoceptive and exteroceptive P3b components

sub_predictions.claim: Thalamic TMS affects ignition probability globally
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Bₜ shift (PCI reduction) across both interoceptive and exteroceptive stimulus streams
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Site-nonspecific effect equivalent across all TMS targets

validation_notes: Prediction structurally sound. Biological substrate specification (thalamic gating) flagged as one plausible implementation among alternatives including cortical inhibition and claustrum. Caption in paper MUST note this is not the sole candidate substrate.

inclusion_criteria: No contraindications to TMS (Rossi et al. 2021 safety checklist)
Available structural T1-weighted MRI for neuronavigation
Resting motor threshold (RMT) determinable at FDI
exclusion_criteria: Personal or first-degree family history of epilepsy or seizure disorder
Metallic implants in head or neck
Cochlear implants or deep brain stimulators
History of significant head injury (loss of consciousness > 5 min)
Irregular cardiac rhythm on screening ECG
participants.tms_screening: Safety checklist per Rossi et al. (2021) Clin. Neurophysiol.; written informed consent to TMS risks

apparatus.TMS_system: Magstim Rapid² with 70 mm figure-of-eight coil (active) and sham coil (angled 90°)
apparatus.neuronavigation: Brainsight 2 (Rogue Research) with individual T1-weighted MRI (1 mm isotropic, MPRAGE)

target_regions.name: Right posterior insula (pIC)
target_regions.rationale: Somatic marker integration and interoceptive precision gating

target_regions.name: Mediodorsal thalamus
target_regions.rationale: Global broadcast and ignition threshold regulation

target_regions.name: Vertex (sham control)
target_regions.rationale: Active control for nonspecific TMS effects

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp (TMS-compatible, Brain Products GmbH) at 5000 Hz, with TMS artifact suppression
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II synchronised to EEG

trial_structure.response: Visibility rating 1–4 (Perceptual Awareness Scale, PAS), response window 2000 ms

blocks.condition_counterbalancing: Latin square across TMS-site × contrast blocks; TMS site blocked to avoid coil repositioning during task

pci_protocol.artifact_suppression: Fastrak suppression + SSP projection + ICA (Hernandez-Pavon 2023 pipeline)
pci_protocol.pci_computation_software: Comolatti et al. (2019) open-source PCI toolbox

dependent_variables: PCI per TMS condition (insula, thalamus, vertex)
HEP amplitude (250–400 ms) per TMS condition
HEP–P3b coupling coefficient per TMS condition
PAS rating distribution per TMS condition
Estimated θₜ shift (fitted APGI model) per TMS condition
Estimated Πⁱ_eff (fitted APGI model) per TMS condition
primary_hypothesis: Active insula TMS reduces PCI and abolishes the HEP–P3b coupling relative to vertex sham, consistent with a TMS-induced reduction in Πⁱ_eff and consequent elevation of θₜ. Thalamic TMS reduces PCI across both interoceptive and exteroceptive stimulus streams.
primary_analysis: Linear mixed-effects: PCI ~ TMS_site * stream + (TMS_site | participant). Ordinal mixed-effects: PAS_rating ~ TMS_site * contrast + (TMS_site | participant). HEP–P3b coupling tested via mediation analysis with TMS_site as moderator.

power_analysis.based_on: Rosanova et al. (2012) TMS–EEG PCI effects; typical insula TMS interoception disruption Cohen's d ≈ 0.5

safety_monitoring.adverse_event_protocol: See SOP-TMS-002; participants screened for headache, phosphenes, and discomfort after each block

caveats: Thalamic gating is one plausible biological implementation of θₜ regulation. Cortical inhibition (layer-specific GABAergic) and claustrum co-activation remain viable alternative substrates. Paper caption in Figure 3 and Table 2 must include the following statement verbatim: 'Thalamic and insular involvement is consistent with APGI threshold predictions but does not exclude cortical inhibitory or claustrum-mediated mechanisms.'
